rationale: The cited work is referenced in the main argument text, not just in a background list, and it underwrites a concrete interpretation in the "Lessons for Our Field" section. Huber explicitly attributes the incentive-compatibility point to Green et al. (2003) and then builds his forward-looking discussion on that premise. Although the citation is brief and not methodological, it plays a meaningful conceptual support role.
relation_description: The citing essay uses Green, Johnson, and Neal (2003) as support for a specific field-level claim that academic and practitioner incentives are becoming incompatible. This prior diagnosis is used to frame the paper's argument that bridging theory and practice will be harder in the future.
